_comment: FILE CREATED BY CAT REALM - MODIFY AT YOUR OWN RISK
name: CatRealm Server
author: admin@nekohosting.gg
description: Self-hosted fantasy chat server. Users connect by IP/domain using the CatRealm client.

installation.script:
#!/bin/bash
# CatRealm Server - Pterodactyl Install Script

apt-get update -y
apt-get install -y curl git python3 make g++ ffmpeg yt-dlp

# Install Node.js 20 if not present
if ! command -v node &> /dev/null; then
  curl -fsSL https://deb.nodesource.com/setup_20.x | bash -
  apt-get install -y nodejs
fi

echo "Node version: $(node -v)"

# Download latest release from GitHub
cd /mnt/server

git config --global --add safe.directory /mnt/server || true

if [ -d ".git" ]; then
  echo "Updating existing installation..."
  git fetch --all --prune || true
  git reset --hard origin/${GIT_BRANCH:-main} || git pull
else
  echo "Fresh install..."
  git clone ${GIT_REPO:-https://github.com/VanillaChan6571/CatRealm-SelfHostable-Server.git} . || {
    echo "Git clone failed - manual install may be required"
    exit 1
  }
  git config --global --add safe.directory /mnt/server || true
  git checkout ${GIT_BRANCH:-main} || true
fi

chmod +x scripts/auto-update-start.sh || true

# Create data directory
mkdir -p /mnt/server/data

# Preserve the existing environment file before rewriting it on reinstall
if [ -f /mnt/server/.env ]; then
  mkdir -p /mnt/server/backup-before-reinstall
  cp /mnt/server/.env "/mnt/server/backup-before-reinstall/.env-$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S)"
fi

# Write .env from Pterodactyl environment variables
cp /mnt/server/.env.example /mnt/server/.env

set_env() {
  python3 - "$1" "$2" /mnt/server/.env <<'PYEOF'
import sys, re
key, val, path = sys.argv[1], sys.argv[2], sys.argv[3]
with open(path) as f: content = f.read()
pattern = re.compile(r'^#*' + re.escape(key) + r'=.*', re.MULTILINE)
if pattern.search(content):
    content = pattern.sub(key + '=' + val, content, count=1)
else:
    content += '\n' + key + '=' + val + '\n'
with open(path, 'w') as f: f.write(content)
PYEOF
}

set_env "PORT"                    "${SERVER_PORT}"
set_env "SERVER_URL"              "http://0.0.0.0:${SERVER_PORT}"
set_env "SERVER_NAME"             "${SERVER_NAME}"
set_env "SERVER_DESCRIPTION"      "${SERVER_DESCRIPTION}"
set_env "REGISTRATION_OPEN"       "${REGISTRATION_OPEN}"
set_env "SERVER_MODE"             "${SERVER_MODE}"
set_env "DB_PATH"                 "/mnt/server/data/catrealm.db"
set_env "CLIENT_URL"              "*"
set_env "TURN_MODE"               "${TURN_MODE}"
set_env "TURN_SECRET"             "${TURN_SECRET}"
set_env "TURN_HOST"               "${TURN_HOST}"
set_env "TURN_PORT"               "${TURN_PORT}"
set_env "TURN_TLS_PORT"           "${TURN_TLS_PORT}"
set_env "TURN_FORCE_TCP"          "${TURN_FORCE_TCP}"
set_env "HOST_LIVEKIT_MEDIA"      "${HOST_LIVEKIT_MEDIA}"
set_env "LIVEKIT_PUBLIC_HOST"     "${LIVEKIT_PUBLIC_HOST}"
set_env "LIVEKIT_SIGNALING_PORT"  "${LIVEKIT_SIGNALING_PORT}"
set_env "LIVEKIT_RTC_TCP_PORT"    "${LIVEKIT_RTC_TCP_PORT}"
set_env "LIVEKIT_RTC_UDP_PORT_START" "${LIVEKIT_RTC_UDP_PORT_START}"
set_env "LIVEKIT_RTC_UDP_PORT_END" "${LIVEKIT_RTC_UDP_PORT_END}"
set_env "MEDIA_LIVEKIT_ENABLED"   "${MEDIA_LIVEKIT_ENABLED}"
set_env "MEDIA_LIVEKIT_URL"       "${MEDIA_LIVEKIT_URL}"
set_env "MEDIA_LIVEKIT_PUBLIC_WS_URL" "${MEDIA_LIVEKIT_PUBLIC_WS_URL}"
set_env "MEDIA_LIVEKIT_API_KEY"   "${MEDIA_LIVEKIT_API_KEY}"
set_env "MEDIA_LIVEKIT_API_SECRET" "${MEDIA_LIVEKIT_API_SECRET}"
set_env "MEDIA_FALLBACK_TO_LEGACY" "${MEDIA_FALLBACK_TO_LEGACY}"
set_env "MEDIA_TOKEN_TTL_SECONDS"  "${MEDIA_TOKEN_TTL_SECONDS}"
set_env "SSL_DOMAIN"              "${SSL_DOMAIN}"
set_env "SSL_EMAIL"               "${SSL_EMAIL}"
set_env "SSL_DNS_PROVIDER"        "${SSL_DNS_PROVIDER}"
set_env "SSL_DNS_API_TOKEN"       "${SSL_DNS_API_TOKEN}"
set_env "SSL_CERT_PATH"           "${SSL_CERT_PATH}"
set_env "SSL_KEY_PATH"            "${SSL_KEY_PATH}"
set_env "AUTO_UPDATE"             "${AUTO_UPDATE}"
set_env "AUTO_UPDATE_RESTART_ON_START" "true"
set_env "GIT_REPO"                "${GIT_REPO}"
set_env "GIT_BRANCH"              "${GIT_BRANCH}"

mkdir -p /mnt/server/ssl
echo "CatRealm installation complete!"

variables.name: Server Name
variables.description: The display name of your chat server (shown to users on the connect screen).

variables.name: Server Description
variables.description: A short description of your server.

variables.name: Open Registration
variables.description: Allow new users to register. Set to 'false' to make the server invite-only.

variables.name: SSL Domain (Auto-SSL)
variables.description: Domain name for automatic Let's Encrypt SSL. Requires the domain to point to this server. Leave empty to disable.

variables.name: SSL Email (Auto-SSL)
variables.description: Contact email for Let's Encrypt certificate notifications. Required if SSL Domain is set.

variables.name: SSL Certificate Path
variables.description: Path to SSL certificate file (e.g. ./ssl/cert.pem). Leave empty for plain HTTP.

variables.name: SSL Key Path
variables.description: Path to SSL private key file (e.g. ./ssl/key.pem). Leave empty for plain HTTP.

variables.name: Server Mode
variables.description: central_only: CatRealm central accounts only; mixed: central + local; decentral_only: local only

variables.name: TURN Mode
variables.description: fallback: public relay, central: central auth TURN, custom: your own coturn

variables.name: TURN Secret (Custom)
variables.description: Used in TURN mode custom. Must match your coturn static-auth-secret.

variables.name: TURN Host (Custom)
variables.description: Used in TURN mode custom. Hostname only (no http/https, no port).

variables.name: TURN Port (Custom)
variables.description: Used in TURN mode custom.

variables.name: TURN TLS Port (Custom)
variables.description: Used in TURN mode custom. Leave blank to disable turns endpoint.

variables.name: TURN Force TCP
variables.description: When true, only TCP TURN endpoints are returned (helps restricted networks).

variables.name: Host LiveKit Media w/ Server
variables.description: Starts bundled livekit-server in the same CatRealm runtime container. Requires the CatRealm Runtime image and LiveKit port allocations.

variables.name: LiveKit Public Host
variables.description: Public hostname clients use for LiveKit. Leave empty to use SSL Domain. Example: media.example.com

variables.name: LiveKit Signaling Port
variables.description: TCP WebSocket/signaling port for bundled LiveKit. Allocate this TCP port in Pterodactyl.

variables.name: LiveKit RTC TCP Port
variables.description: TCP fallback RTC port for bundled LiveKit. Allocate this TCP port in Pterodactyl.

variables.name: LiveKit UDP Port Start
variables.description: Start of bundled LiveKit UDP media range. Allocate the full UDP range in Pterodactyl/firewall.

variables.name: LiveKit UDP Port End
variables.description: End of bundled LiveKit UDP media range. Small servers can use 50000-50100; larger servers need more ports.

variables.name: External LiveKit Enabled
variables.description: Advanced: enable an external LiveKit server instead of the bundled sidecar. Bundled mode sets this automatically.

variables.name: External LiveKit Internal URL
variables.description: Advanced: CatRealm-to-LiveKit URL for external LiveKit, e.g. ws://10.0.0.5:7880 or wss://media.example.com.

variables.name: External LiveKit Public WS URL
variables.description: Advanced: client-facing external LiveKit URL, usually wss://media.example.com.

variables.name: LiveKit API Key
variables.description: API key configured on the LiveKit sidecar.

variables.name: LiveKit API Secret
variables.description: API secret configured on the LiveKit sidecar. Keep this private.

variables.name: LiveKit Fallback To Legacy
variables.description: When true, clients fall back to legacy voice if LiveKit is unavailable or disabled.

variables.name: LiveKit Token TTL Seconds
variables.description: Lifetime for CatRealm-issued LiveKit access tokens.

variables.name: Auto Update
variables.description: If true, startup will fetch and reset to origin branch when this server folder is a git repository.

variables.name: Git Repository URL
variables.description: Repository used for install/update. Leave default unless you forked CatRealm.

variables.name: Git Branch
variables.description: Branch used for install/update.

variables.name: SSL DNS Provider
variables.description: DNS provider for DNS-01 challenge (no port 80 needed). Supported: cloudflare. Leave empty to use HTTP-01 challenge on port 80.

variables.name: SSL DNS API Token
variables.description: API token for the DNS provider. For Cloudflare: create a token with Zone:DNS:Edit permissions.
